records.ety: [L. emergere, emersum; e out + mergere to dip, plunge. See Merge.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Emerged (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Emerging (?).]

defs: To rise out of a fluid; to come forth from that in which anything has been plunged, enveloped, or concealed; to issue and appear; as, to emerge from the water or the ocean; the sun emerges from behind the moon in an eclipse; to emerge from poverty or obscurity.
quotes: Those who have emerged from very low, some from the lowest, classes of society.
